subject: Re: beta 1.0.7W crashes immediatly
body:
Dear Yong-Hui,

I observed this error once myself, and it was gone after rebooting the 
system. Unfortunately, I haven't been able to reproduce it since then. 
Please let me know if rebooting helps.

Starting with Windows 10, the new GPU driver model allocates a significant 
amount of memory for the OS, leading to the decrease in available memory 
you see. However, on Quadro and Tesla cards (and some old Titan models, I 
think) that are not used for video output, you can switch to TCC mode to 
get back those 1.2 GB. Here is how: 
https://docs.nvidia.com/gameworks/content/developertools/desktop/nsight/tesla_compute_cluster.htm. 
Alternatively, Windows 7 consumes less memory, but will reach its end of 
life by the end of this year.

Cheers,
Dimitry


On Tuesday, May 28, 2019 at 7:34:54 AM UTC+2, Yong-Hui Sung wrote:
>
> Hi,
>
> I had trouble with K3 data. Thus I downloaded the beta 1.07W_beta2. 
> However as soon as I start processing I am getting: 
>
> System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of 
> an object.
>
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.SendCommand(NamedSerializableObject command)
>
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Dispose()
>
>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.Finalize()
>
> Further I noticed our QuadroP6000 cards are shown as having 6800 MB RAM 
> but they are supposed to have 8000 MB. 
>
> Any idea what is wrong. 
>
>
> best
>
> YHS
>
